concept.title: Deterministic Migration vs AI Conversion
concept.shortdesc: Use deterministic migration to make structure and execution outcomes inspectable before you apply changes.

conbody.p: AI conversion can generate DITA quickly, but generated content often carries structural variance that blocks predictable downstream publishing. Deterministic migration complements AI conversion by enforcing explicit review points and explicit mutation contracts.

strow.stentry: Dry-run by default, apply only with --apply.

section.title: Recommended workflow
section.p: Use AI conversion to create a first-pass package, then use this processor to normalize structure, validate assumptions, and produce an auditable migration output. This reduces ambiguity when multiple engineers or teams work in parallel.
